message:
test(extension_preflight): comprehensive unit tests for security rulebook v2.0.0 detection rules

Add 396 lines of targeted unit tests covering all 9 new security rules
introduced in rulebook v2.0.0:

- SEC-SPAWN-001 (ChildProcessSpawn): exec, spawn, fork detection + negative
  case ensuring plain exec() without child_process context is not flagged
- SEC-CONSTRUCTOR-001 (ConstructorEscape): dot-notation and bracket-notation
  constructor.constructor('return this')() sandbox breakout patterns
- SEC-NATIVEMOD-001 (NativeModuleRequire): .node, .so, .dylib native addon
  loading detection + negative case for standard require('fs')
- SEC-GLOBAL-001 (GlobalMutation): globalThis.X = ..., global.X = ...,
  and bracket-notation assignment detection + negative for reads
- SEC-SYMLINK-001 (SymlinkCreation): fs.symlinkSync and fs.linkSync
- SEC-CHMOD-001 (PermissionChange): fs.chmodSync and fs.chown
- SEC-SOCKET-001 (SocketListener): http.createServer and dgram.createSocket
- SEC-WASM-001 (WebAssemblyUsage): WebAssembly.instantiate and .compile
- SEC-ARGUMENTS-001 (ArgumentsCallerAccess): arguments.callee and .caller

Also tests:
- Serde roundtrip stability for all 9 new SecurityRuleId variants
- Stable serialization names (SEC-SPAWN-001, SEC-CONSTRUCTOR-001, etc.)
- Deterministic scan output when multiple new rules trigger simultaneously
- Deterministic sort ordering within same risk tier (by file, line, column)
- Evidence ledger JSONL emission with rulebook version 2.0.0 tagging
- Default tier consistency (Critical for spawn/constructor/native, High for
  global/symlink/chmod/socket/wasm, Medium for arguments)
- Install-time composite risk classifier integration with new rules
- Comment-awareness: single-line and block comments skip new rule detection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
